先听总故事

Plugin Internals

这一页像一本关于“新本领插件 Internals”的小绘本，它先告诉我们这位机器人朋友会做什么，再一步一步解释什么时候该这样做。 你可以先把它想成：新本领插件 Internals。

原始路径：/plugins/architecture 章节数量：39 查看原文

第 1 站

Start Here

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Start Here”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“新本领插件 Internals”。

原文小纸条

# Plugin Internals

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Info> This is the **deep architecture reference**. For practical guides, see:”。

<Info> This is the **deep architecture reference**. For practical guides, see:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“</Info>”。

原文小纸条

</Info>

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Start Here”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Install and use 新本领插件s — user guide、Getting Started — first 新本领插件 tutorial、消息通道 新本领插件s — build a messaging 消息通道、Provider 新本领插件s — build a 聪明脑袋模型 provider。

原文小纸条
  • Install and use plugins — user guide
  • Getting Started — first plugin tutorial
  • Channel Plugins — build a messaging channel
  • Provider Plugins — build a model provider
  • SDK Overview — import map and registration API
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“This page covers the internal architecture of the OpenClaw 新本领插件 system.”。

原文小纸条

This page covers the internal architecture of the OpenClaw plugin system.

第 2 站

Public capability 聪明脑袋模型

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Public capability 聪明脑袋模型”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Capabilities are the public **native 新本领插件** 聪明脑袋模型 inside OpenClaw. Every native OpenClaw 新本领插件 registers against one…”。

原文小纸条

Capabilities are the public **native plugin** model inside OpenClaw. Every native OpenClaw plugin registers against one or more capability types:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Capability Registration method Example 新本领插件s --------------------- --------------------------------------------- -----…”。

原文小纸条

| Capability | Registration method | Example plugins | | --------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | | Text inference | api.registerProvider(...) | openai, anthropic | | CLI inference backend | api.registerCliBackend(...) | openai, anthropic | | Speech | api.registerSpeechProvider(...) | elevenlabs, microsoft | | Media understanding | api.registerMediaUnderstandingProvider(...) | openai, google | | Image generation | api.registerImageGenerationProvider(...) | openai, google | | Web search | api.registerWebSearchProvider(...) | google | | Channel / messaging | api.registerChannel(...) | msteams, matrix |

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“A 新本领插件 that registers zero capabilities but provides hooks, 工具小帮手s, or services is a **legacy hook-only** 新本领插件. That…”。

原文小纸条

A plugin that registers zero capabilities but provides hooks, tools, or services is a **legacy hook-only** plugin. That pattern is still fully supported.

第 3 站

External compatibility stance

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“External compatibility stance”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“The capability 聪明脑袋模型 is landed in core and used by bundled/native 新本领插件s today, but external 新本领插件 compatibility still…”。

原文小纸条

The capability model is landed in core and used by bundled/native plugins today, but external plugin compatibility still needs a tighter bar than "it is exported, therefore it is frozen."

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Current guidance:”。

原文小纸条

Current guidance:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“this as the compatibility baseline”。

原文小纸条

this as the compatibility baseline

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“vendor-specific reach-ins or new hook-only designs”。

原文小纸条

The before_agent_start hook remains supported as a compatibility path for hook-only plugins. Legacy real-world plugins still depend on it.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Legacy hooks”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：keep it working、document it as legacy、prefer before_聪明脑袋模型_resolve for 聪明脑袋模型/p…、prefer before_prompt_build for prompt mut…。

原文小纸条
  • keep it working
  • document it as legacy
  • prefer before_model_resolve for model/provider override work
  • prefer before_prompt_build for prompt mutation work
  • remove only after real usage drops and fixture coverage proves migration safety

第 6 站

Compatibility signals

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Compatibility signals”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“When you run openclaw doctor or openclaw 新本领插件s inspect <id>, you may see one of these labels:”。

原文小纸条

When you run openclaw doctor or openclaw plugins inspect <id>, you may see one of these labels:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Signal Meaning -------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------ **设置说明书 valid**…”。

原文小纸条

| Signal | Meaning | | -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | | **config valid** | Config parses fine and plugins resolve | | **compatibility advisory** | Plugin uses a supported-but-older pattern (e.g. hook-only) | | **legacy warning** | Plugin uses before_agent_start, which is deprecated | | **hard error** | Config is invalid or plugin failed to load |

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Neither hook-only nor before_机器人朋友_start will break your 新本领插件 today -- hook-only is advisory, and before_机器人朋友_start o…”。

原文小纸条

Neither hook-only nor before_agent_start will break your plugin today -- hook-only is advisory, and before_agent_start only triggers a warning. These signals also appear in openclaw status --all and openclaw plugins doctor.

第 7 站

Architecture overview

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Architecture overview”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“OpenClaw's 新本领插件 system has four layers:”。

原文小纸条

OpenClaw's plugin system has four layers:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. **Manifest + discovery** OpenClaw finds candidate 新本领插件s from 设置说明书ured paths, workspace roots, global extension roo…”。

原文小纸条

1. **Manifest + discovery** OpenClaw finds candidate plugins from configured paths, workspace roots, global extension roots, and bundled extensions. Discovery reads native openclaw.plugin.json manifests plus supported bundle manifests first. 2. **Enablement + validation** Core decides whether a discovered plugin is enabled, disabled, blocked, or selected for an exclusive slot such as memory. 3. **Runtime loading** Native OpenClaw plugins are loaded in-process via jiti and register capabilities into a central registry. Compatible bundles are normalized into registry records without importing runtime code. 4. **Surface consumption** The rest of OpenClaw reads the registry to expose tools, channels, provider setup, hooks, HTTP routes, CLI commands, and services.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“The important design boundary:”。

原文小纸条

The important design boundary:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“without executing 新本领插件 code”。

原文小纸条

without executing plugin code

原文小纸条
  • discovery + config validation should work from **manifest/schema metadata**
  • native runtime behavior comes from the plugin module's register(api) path
像讲绘本

原文小纸条

That split lets OpenClaw validate config, explain missing/disabled plugins, and build UI/schema hints before the full runtime is active.

第 8 站

消息通道 新本领插件s and the shared 小纸条消息 工具小帮手

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“消息通道 新本领插件s and the shared 小纸条消息 工具小帮手”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“消息通道 新本领插件s do not need to register a separate send/edit/react 工具小帮手 for normal chat actions. OpenClaw keeps one shared…”。

原文小纸条

Channel plugins do not need to register a separate send/edit/react tool for normal chat actions. OpenClaw keeps one shared message tool in core, and channel plugins own the channel-specific discovery and execution behind it.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Channel plugins and the shared message tool”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：core owns the shared 小纸条消息 工具小帮手 host, pr…、消息通道 新本领插件s own scoped action discovery,…、消息通道 新本领插件s execute the final action thro…。

原文小纸条
  • core owns the shared message tool host, prompt wiring, session/thread
  • channel plugins own scoped action discovery, capability discovery, and any
  • channel plugins execute the final action through their action adapter
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“For 消息通道 新本领插件s, the SDK surface is 消息通道小纸条消息ActionAdapter.describe小纸条消息工具小帮手(...). That unified discovery call lets a…”。

原文小纸条

For channel plugins, the SDK surface is ChannelMessageActionAdapter.describeMessageTool(...). That unified discovery call lets a plugin return its visible actions, capabilities, and schema contributions together so those pieces do not drift apart.

  • currentChannelId

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“That matters for context-sensitive 新本领插件s. A 消息通道 can hide or expose 小纸条消息 actions based on the active account, current…”。

原文小纸条

That matters for context-sensitive plugins. A channel can hide or expose message actions based on the active account, current room/thread/message, or trusted requester identity without hardcoding channel-specific branches in the core message tool.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“This is why embedded-runner routing changes are still 新本领插件 work: the runner is responsible for forwarding the current…”。

原文小纸条

This is why embedded-runner routing changes are still plugin work: the runner is responsible for forwarding the current chat/session identity into the plugin discovery boundary so the shared message tool exposes the right channel-owned surface for the current turn.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“For 消息通道-owned execution helpers, bundled 新本领插件s should keep the execution runtime inside their own extension modules.…”。

原文小纸条

For channel-owned execution helpers, bundled plugins should keep the execution runtime inside their own extension modules. Core no longer owns the Discord, Slack, Telegram, or WhatsApp message-action runtimes under src/agents/tools. We do not publish separate plugin-sdk/*-action-runtime subpaths, and bundled plugins should import their own local runtime code directly from their extension-owned modules.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Core now defers shared poll parsing until after 新本领插件 poll dispatch declines the action, so 新本领插件-owned poll handlers c…”。

原文小纸条

Core now defers shared poll parsing until after plugin poll dispatch declines the action, so plugin-owned poll handlers can accept channel-specific poll fields without being blocked by the generic poll parser first.

  • a company plugin should usually own all of that company's OpenClaw-facing
  • a feature plugin should usually own the full feature surface it introduces
  • channels should consume shared core capabilities instead of re-implementing

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Capability ownership model”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：the bundled openai 新本领插件 owns OpenAI 聪明脑袋…、the bundled elevenlabs 新本领插件 owns ElevenL…、the bundled microsoft 新本领插件 owns Microsof…、the bundled google 新本领插件 owns Google 聪明脑袋…。

原文小纸条
  • the bundled openai plugin owns OpenAI model-provider behavior and OpenAI
  • the bundled elevenlabs plugin owns ElevenLabs speech behavior
  • the bundled microsoft plugin owns Microsoft speech behavior
  • the bundled google plugin owns Google model-provider behavior plus Google
  • the bundled minimax, mistral, moonshot, and zai plugins own their
  • the voice-call plugin is a feature plugin: it owns call transport, tools,

  • another vendor can do the same for its own surface area
  • channels do not care which vendor plugin owns the provider; they consume the

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“So if OpenClaw adds a new domain such as video, the first question is not "which provider should hardcode video handlin…”。

原文小纸条

So if OpenClaw adds a new domain such as video, the first question is not "which provider should hardcode video handling?" The first question is "what is the core video capability contract?" Once that contract exists, vendor plugins can register against it and channel/feature plugins can consume it.

原文小纸条

1. define the missing capability in core 2. expose it through the plugin API/runtime in a typed way 3. wire channels/features against that capability 4. let vendor plugins register implementations

This keeps ownership explicit while avoiding core behavior that depends on a single vendor or a one-off plugin-specific code path.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Capability layering”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**core capability layer**: shared orchest…、**vendor 新本领插件 layer**: vendor-specific 对…、**消息通道/feature 新本领插件 layer**: Slack/Disco…。

原文小纸条
  • **core capability layer**: shared orchestration, policy, fallback, config
  • **vendor plugin layer**: vendor-specific APIs, auth, model catalogs, speech
  • **channel/feature plugin layer**: Slack/Discord/voice-call/etc. integration

像准备清单

原文小纸条
  • core owns reply-time TTS policy, fallback order, prefs, and channel delivery
  • openai, elevenlabs, and microsoft own synthesis implementations
  • voice-call consumes the telephony TTS runtime helper

第 11 站

Multi-capability company 新本领插件 example

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Multi-capability company 新本领插件 example”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“A company 新本领插件 should feel cohesive from the outside. If OpenClaw has shared contracts for 聪明脑袋模型s, speech, media unde…”。

原文小纸条

A company plugin should feel cohesive from the outside. If OpenClaw has shared contracts for models, speech, media understanding, and web search, a vendor can own all of its surfaces in one place:

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“import type { OpenClawPluginDefinition } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk";”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这一行“import {”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这一行“buildOpenAISpeechProvider,”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
原文代码块
import type { OpenClawPluginDefinition } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk";
import {
  buildOpenAISpeechProvider,
  createPluginBackedWebSearchProvider,
  describeImageWithModel,
  transcribeOpenAiCompatibleAudio,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk";

const plugin: OpenClawPluginDefinition = {
  id: "exampleai",
  name: "ExampleAI",
  register(api) {
    api.registerProvider({
      id: "exampleai",
      // auth/model catalog/runtime hooks
    });

    api.registerSpeechProvider(
      buildOpenAISpeechProvider({
        id: "exampleai",
        // vendor speech config
      }),
    );

    api.registerMediaUnderstandingProvider({
      id: "exampleai",
      capabilities: ["image", "audio", "video"],
      async describeImage(req) {
        return describeImageWithModel({
          provider: "exampleai",
          model: req.model,
          input: req.input,
        });
      },
      async transcribeAudio(req) {
        return transcribeOpenAiCompatibleAudio({
          provider: "exampleai",
          model: req.model,
          input: req.input,
        });
      },
    });

    api.registerWebSearchProvider(
      createPluginBackedWebSearchProvider({
        id: "exampleai-search",
        // credential + fetch logic
      }),
    );
  },
};

export default plugin;
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“What matters is not the exact helper names. The shape matters:”。

原文小纸条

What matters is not the exact helper names. The shape matters:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Multi-capability company plugin example”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：one 新本领插件 owns the vendor surface、core still owns the capability contracts、消息通道s and feature 新本领插件s consume 对话接口.run…、contract tests can assert that the 新本领插件…。

原文小纸条
  • one plugin owns the vendor surface
  • core still owns the capability contracts
  • channels and feature plugins consume api.runtime.* helpers, not vendor code
  • contract tests can assert that the plugin registered the capabilities it

原文小纸条

That avoids baking one provider's video assumptions into core. The plugin owns the vendor surface; core owns the capability contract and fallback behavior.

像讲绘本

原文小纸条

If OpenClaw adds a new domain later, such as video generation, use the same sequence again: define the core capability first, then let vendor plugins register implementations against it.

第 13 站

Contracts and enforcement

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Contracts and enforcement”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“The 新本领插件 对话接口 surface is intentionally typed and centralized in OpenClaw新本领插件对话接口. That contract defines the supported…”。

原文小纸条

The plugin API surface is intentionally typed and centralized in OpenClawPluginApi. That contract defines the supported registration points and the runtime helpers a plugin may rely on.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Why this matters:”。

Why this matters:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“provider id”。

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Contracts and enforcement”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：新本领插件 authors get one stable internal sta…、core can reject duplicate ownership such…、startup can surface actionable diagnostic…、contract tests can enforce bundled-新本领插件…。

原文小纸条
  • plugin authors get one stable internal standard
  • core can reject duplicate ownership such as two plugins registering the same
  • startup can surface actionable diagnostics for malformed registration
  • contract tests can enforce bundled-plugin ownership and prevent silent drift
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“There are two layers of enforcement:”。

原文小纸条

There are two layers of enforcement:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. **runtime registration enforcement** The 新本领插件 registry validates registrations as 新本领插件s load. Examples: duplicate…”。

原文小纸条

1. **runtime registration enforcement** The plugin registry validates registrations as plugins load. Examples: duplicate provider ids, duplicate speech provider ids, and malformed registrations produce plugin diagnostics instead of undefined behavior. 2. **contract tests** Bundled plugins are captured in contract registries during test runs so OpenClaw can assert ownership explicitly. Today this is used for model providers, speech providers, web search providers, and bundled registration ownership.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“The practical effect is that OpenClaw knows, up front, which 新本领插件 owns which surface. That lets core and 消息通道s compose…”。

原文小纸条

The practical effect is that OpenClaw knows, up front, which plugin owns which surface. That lets core and channels compose seamlessly because ownership is declared, typed, and testable rather than implicit.

第 14 站

What belongs in a contract

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“What belongs in a contract”。

像准备清单

这一组条目像“What belongs in a contract”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：typed、small、capability-specific、owned by core。

原文小纸条
  • typed
  • small
  • capability-specific
  • owned by core
  • reusable by multiple plugins
  • consumable by channels/features without vendor knowledge
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Bad 新本领插件 contracts are:”。

像准备清单

这一组条目像“What belongs in a contract”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：vendor-specific policy hidden in core、one-off 新本领插件 escape hatches that bypass…、消息通道 code reaching straight into a vendor…、ad hoc runtime objects that are not part…。

原文小纸条
  • vendor-specific policy hidden in core
  • one-off plugin escape hatches that bypass the registry
  • channel code reaching straight into a vendor implementation
  • ad hoc runtime objects that are not part of OpenClawPluginApi or
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“When in doubt, raise the abstraction level: define the capability first, then let 新本领插件s plug into it.”。

原文小纸条

When in doubt, raise the abstraction level: define the capability first, then let plugins plug into it.

第 15 站

Execution 聪明脑袋模型

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Execution 聪明脑袋模型”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Native OpenClaw 新本领插件s run **in-process** with the 门口的小门卫. They are not sandboxed. A loaded native 新本领插件 has the same p…”。

原文小纸条

Native OpenClaw plugins run **in-process** with the Gateway. They are not sandboxed. A loaded native plugin has the same process-level trust boundary as core code.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Implications:”。

原文小纸条

Implications:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“the OpenClaw process”。

原文小纸条

the OpenClaw process

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Execution model”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：a native 新本领插件 can register 工具小帮手s, netwo…、a native 新本领插件 bug can crash or destabili…、a malicious native 新本领插件 is equivalent to…。

原文小纸条
  • a native plugin can register tools, network handlers, hooks, and services
  • a native plugin bug can crash or destabilize the gateway
  • a malicious native plugin is equivalent to arbitrary code execution inside
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Compatible bundles are safer by default because OpenClaw currently treats them as metadata/content packs. In current re…”。

原文小纸条

Compatible bundles are safer by default because OpenClaw currently treats them as metadata/content packs. In current releases, that mostly means bundled skills.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Use allowlists and explicit install/load paths for non-bundled 新本领插件s. Treat workspace 新本领插件s as development-time code,…”。

原文小纸条

Use allowlists and explicit install/load paths for non-bundled plugins. Treat workspace plugins as development-time code, not production defaults.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“For bundled workspace package names, keep the 新本领插件 id anchored in the npm name: @openclaw/<id> by default, or an appro…”。

原文小纸条

For bundled workspace package names, keep the plugin id anchored in the npm name: @openclaw/<id> by default, or an approved typed suffix such as -provider, -plugin, -speech, -sandbox, or -media-understanding when the package intentionally exposes a narrower plugin role.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Important trust note:”。

原文小纸条

Important trust note:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“the bundled copy when that workspace 新本领插件 is enabled/allowlisted.”。

原文小纸条

the bundled copy when that workspace plugin is enabled/allowlisted.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Execution model”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：新本领插件s.allow trusts **新本领插件 ids**, not so…、A workspace 新本领插件 with the same id as a b…、This is normal and useful for local devel…。

原文小纸条
  • plugins.allow trusts **plugin ids**, not source provenance.
  • A workspace plugin with the same id as a bundled plugin intentionally shadows
  • This is normal and useful for local development, patch testing, and hotfixes.

第 16 站

Export boundary

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Export boundary”。

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“OpenClaw exports capabilities, not implementation convenience.”。

原文小纸条

OpenClaw exports capabilities, not implementation convenience.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Keep capability registration public. Trim non-contract helper exports:”。

原文小纸条

Keep capability registration public. Trim non-contract helper exports:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Export boundary”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：bundled-新本领插件-specific helper subpaths、runtime plumbing subpaths not intended as…、vendor-specific convenience helpers、setup/onboarding helpers that are impleme…。

原文小纸条
  • bundled-plugin-specific helper subpaths
  • runtime plumbing subpaths not intended as public API
  • vendor-specific convenience helpers
  • setup/onboarding helpers that are implementation details

第 17 站

Load pipeline

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Load pipeline”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“At startup, OpenClaw does roughly this:”。

原文小纸条

At startup, OpenClaw does roughly this:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. discover candidate 新本领插件 roots 2. read native or compatible bundle manifests and package metadata 3. reject unsafe c…”。

原文小纸条

1. discover candidate plugin roots 2. read native or compatible bundle manifests and package metadata 3. reject unsafe candidates 4. normalize plugin config (plugins.enabled, allow, deny, entries, slots, load.paths) 5. decide enablement for each candidate 6. load enabled native modules via jiti 7. call native register(api) hooks and collect registrations into the plugin registry 8. expose the registry to commands/runtime surfaces

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“The safety gates happen **before** runtime execution. Candidates are blocked when the entry escapes the 新本领插件 root, the…”。

原文小纸条

The safety gates happen **before** runtime execution. Candidates are blocked when the entry escapes the plugin root, the path is world-writable, or path ownership looks suspicious for non-bundled plugins.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“For native 新本领插件s, the runtime module is the data-plane part. It registers actual behavior such as hooks, 工具小帮手s, 魔法口令s…”。

原文小纸条

For native plugins, the runtime module is the data-plane part. It registers actual behavior such as hooks, tools, commands, or provider flows.

Loaded plugins do not directly mutate random core globals. They register into a central plugin registry.

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“The registry tracks:”。

原文小纸条

Core features then read from that registry instead of talking to plugin modules directly. This keeps loading one-way:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Registry model”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：新本领插件 module -> registry registration、core runtime -> registry consumption。

  • core runtime -> registry consumption
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“That separation matters for maintainability. It means most core surfaces only need one integration point: "read the reg…”。

原文小纸条

That separation matters for maintainability. It means most core surfaces only need one integration point: "read the registry", not "special-case every plugin module".

Conversation binding callbacks

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“新本领插件s that bind a conversation can react when an approval is resolved.”。

原文小纸条

Plugins that bind a conversation can react when an approval is resolved.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Use 对话接口.onConversationBindingResolved(...) to receive a callback after a bind request is approved or denied:”。

原文小纸条

Use api.onConversationBindingResolved(...) to receive a callback after a bind request is approved or denied:

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“export default {”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这里在给“id”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“my-plugin”。
  • 这一行“register(api) {”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
原文代码块
export default {
  id: "my-plugin",
  register(api) {
    api.onConversationBindingResolved(async (event) => {
      if (event.status === "approved") {
        // A binding now exists for this plugin + conversation.
        console.log(event.binding?.conversationId);
        return;
      }

      // The request was denied; clear any local pending state.
      console.log(event.request.conversation.conversationId);
    });
  },
};
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Callback payload fields:”。

原文小纸条

Callback payload fields:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“conversation metadata”。

原文小纸条

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Conversation binding callbacks”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：status: "approved" or "denied"、decision: "allow-once", "allow-always", o…、binding: the resolved binding for approve…、request: the original request summary, de…。

原文小纸条
  • status: "approved" or "denied"
  • decision: "allow-once", "allow-always", or "deny"
  • binding: the resolved binding for approved requests
  • request: the original request summary, detach hint, sender id, and
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“This callback is notification-only. It does not change who is allowed to bind a conversation, and it runs after core ap…”。

原文小纸条

This callback is notification-only. It does not change who is allowed to bind a conversation, and it runs after core approval handling finishes.

Provider runtime hooks

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Provider 新本领插件s now have two layers:”。

原文小纸条

Provider plugins now have two layers:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“runtime load, plus providerAuthChoices for cheap onboarding/auth-choice labels and CLI flag metadata before runtime load”。

原文小纸条

runtime load, plus providerAuthChoices for cheap onboarding/auth-choice labels and CLI flag metadata before runtime load

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Provider runtime hooks”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：manifest metadata: providerAuthEnvVars fo…、设置说明书-time hooks: catalog / legacy discov…、runtime hooks: resolveDynamic聪明脑袋模型, prep…。

原文小纸条
  • manifest metadata: providerAuthEnvVars for cheap env-auth lookup before
  • config-time hooks: catalog / legacy discovery
  • runtime hooks: resolveDynamicModel, prepareDynamicModel, normalizeResolvedModel, capabilities, prepareExtraParams, wrapStreamFn, formatApiKey, refreshOAuth, buildAuthDoctorHint, isCacheTtlEligible, buildMissingAuthMessage, suppressBuiltInModel, augmentModelCatalog, isBinaryThinking, supportsXHighThinking, resolveDefaultThinkingLevel, isModernModelRef, prepareRuntimeAuth, resolveUsageAuth, fetchUsageSnapshot
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“OpenClaw still owns the generic 机器人朋友 loop, failover, transcript handling, and 工具小帮手 policy. These hooks are the extens…”。

原文小纸条

OpenClaw still owns the generic agent loop, failover, transcript handling, and tool policy. These hooks are the extension surface for provider-specific behavior without needing a whole custom inference transport.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Use manifest providerAuthEnvVars when the provider has env-based credentials that generic auth/status/聪明脑袋模型-picker pat…”。

原文小纸条

Use manifest providerAuthEnvVars when the provider has env-based credentials that generic auth/status/model-picker paths should see without loading plugin runtime. Use manifest providerAuthChoices when onboarding/auth-choice CLI surfaces should know the provider's choice id, group labels, and simple one-flag auth wiring without loading provider runtime. Keep provider runtime envVars for operator-facing hints such as onboarding labels or OAuth client-id/client-secret setup vars.

第 23 站

Hook order and usage

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Hook order and usage”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“For 聪明脑袋模型/provider 新本领插件s, OpenClaw calls hooks in this rough order. The "When to use" column is the quick decision gu…”。

原文小纸条

For model/provider plugins, OpenClaw calls hooks in this rough order. The "When to use" column is the quick decision guide.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Hook What it does When to use -- ----------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------…”。

原文小纸条

| # | Hook | What it does | When to use | | -- | ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | 1 | catalog | Publish provider config into models.providers during models.json generation | Provider owns a catalog or base URL defaults | | -- | *(built-in model lookup)* | OpenClaw tries the normal registry/catalog path first | *(not a plugin hook)* | | 2 | resolveDynamicModel | Sync fallback for provider-owned model ids not in the local registry yet | Provider accepts arbitrary upstream model ids | | 3 | prepareDynamicModel | Async warm-up, then resolveDynamicModel runs again | Provider needs network metadata before resolving unknown ids | | 4 | normalizeResolvedModel | Final rewrite before the embedded runner uses the resolved model | Provider needs transport rewrites but still uses a core transport | | 5 | capabilities | Provider-owned transcript/tooling metadata used by shared core logic | Provider needs transcript/provider-family quirks | | 6 | prepareExtraParams | Request-param normalization before generic stream option wrappers | Provider needs default request params or per-provider param cleanup | | 7 | wrapStreamFn | Stream wrapper after generic wrappers are applied | Provider needs request headers/body/model compat wrappers without a custom transport | | 8 | formatApiKey | Auth-profile formatter: stored profile becomes the runtime apiKey string | Provider stores extra auth metadata and needs a custom runtime token shape | | 9 | refreshOAuth | OAuth refresh override for custom refresh endpoints or refresh-failure policy | Provider does not fit the shared pi-ai refreshers | | 10 | buildAuthDoctorHint | Repair hint appended when OAuth refresh fails | Provider needs provider-owned auth repair guidance after refresh failure | | 11 | isCacheTtlEligible | Prompt-cache policy for proxy/backhaul providers | Provider needs proxy-specific cache TTL gating | | 12 | buildMissingAuthMessage | Replacement for the generic missing-auth recovery message | Provider needs a provider-specific missing-auth recovery hint | | 13 | suppressBuiltInModel | Stale upstream model suppression plus optional user-facing error hint | Provider needs to hide stale upstream rows or replace them with a vendor hint | | 14 | augmentModelCatalog | Synthetic/final catalog rows appended after discovery | Provider needs synthetic forward-compat rows in models list and pickers | | 15 | isBinaryThinking | On/off reasoning toggle for binary-thinking providers | Provider exposes only binary thinking on/off | | 16 | supportsXHighThinking | xhigh reasoning support for selected models | Provider wants xhigh on only a subset of models | | 17 | resolveDefaultThinkingLevel | Default /think level for a specific model family | Provider owns default /think policy for a model family | | 18 | isModernModelRef | Modern-model matcher for live profile filters and smoke selection | Provider owns live/smoke preferred-model matching | | 19 | prepareRuntimeAuth | Exchange a configured credential into the actual runtime token/key just before inference | Provider needs a token exchange or short-lived request credential | | 20 | resolveUsageAuth | Resolve usage/billing credentials for /usage and related status surfaces | Provider needs custom usage/quota token parsing or a different usage credential | | 21 | fetchUsageSnapshot | Fetch and normalize provider-specific usage/quota snapshots after auth is resolved | Provider needs a provider-specific usage endpoint or payload parser |

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If the provider needs a fully custom wire protocol or custom request executor, that is a different class of extension.…”。

原文小纸条

If the provider needs a fully custom wire protocol or custom request executor, that is a different class of extension. These hooks are for provider behavior that still runs on OpenClaw's normal inference loop.

第 24 站

Provider example

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Provider example”。

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“api.registerProvider({”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这里在给“id”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“example-proxy”。
  • 这里在给“label”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“Example Proxy”。
原文代码块
api.registerProvider({
  id: "example-proxy",
  label: "Example Proxy",
  auth: [],
  catalog: {
    order: "simple",
    run: async (ctx) => {
      const apiKey = ctx.resolveProviderApiKey("example-proxy").apiKey;
      if (!apiKey) {
        return null;
      }
      return {
        provider: {
          baseUrl: "https://proxy.example.com/v1",
          apiKey,
          api: "openai-completions",
          models: [{ id: "auto", name: "Auto" }],
        },
      };
    },
  },
  resolveDynamicModel: (ctx) => ({
    id: ctx.modelId,
    name: ctx.modelId,
    provider: "example-proxy",
    api: "openai-completions",
    baseUrl: "https://proxy.example.com/v1",
    reasoning: false,
    input: ["text"],
    cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 },
    contextWindow: 128000,
    maxTokens: 8192,
  }),
  prepareRuntimeAuth: async (ctx) => {
    const exchanged = await exchangeToken(ctx.apiKey);
    return {
      apiKey: exchanged.token,
      baseUrl: exchanged.baseUrl,
      expiresAt: exchanged.expiresAt,
    };
  },
  resolveUsageAuth: async (ctx) => {
    const auth = await ctx.resolveOAuthToken();
    return auth ? { token: auth.token } : null;
  },
  fetchUsageSnapshot: async (ctx) => {
    return await fetchExampleProxyUsage(ctx.token, ctx.timeoutMs, ctx.fetchFn);
  },
});

第 25 站

Built-in examples

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Built-in examples”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“resolveUsageAuth, fetchUsageSnapshot, isCacheTtlEligible, resolveDefaultThinkingLevel, and isModern聪明脑袋模型Ref because it…”。

原文小纸条

resolveUsageAuth, fetchUsageSnapshot, isCacheTtlEligible, resolveDefaultThinkingLevel, and isModernModelRef because it owns Claude 4.6 forward-compat, provider-family hints, auth repair guidance, usage endpoint integration, prompt-cache eligibility, and Claude default/adaptive thinking policy.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“capabilities plus buildMissingAuth小纸条消息, suppressBuiltIn聪明脑袋模型, augment聪明脑袋模型Catalog, supportsXHighThinking, and isMode…”。

原文小纸条

capabilities plus buildMissingAuthMessage, suppressBuiltInModel, augmentModelCatalog, supportsXHighThinking, and isModernModelRef because it owns GPT-5.4 forward-compat, the direct OpenAI openai-completions -> openai-responses normalization, Codex-aware auth hints, Spark suppression, synthetic OpenAI list rows, and GPT-5 thinking / live-model policy.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“prepareDynamic聪明脑袋模型 because the provider is pass-through and may expose new 聪明脑袋模型 ids before OpenClaw's static catalo…”。

原文小纸条

prepareDynamicModel because the provider is pass-through and may expose new model ids before OpenClaw's static catalog updates; it also uses capabilities, wrapStreamFn, and isCacheTtlEligible to keep provider-specific request headers, routing metadata, reasoning patches, and prompt-cache policy out of core.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“capabilities plus prepareRuntimeAuth and fetchUsageSnapshot because it needs provider-owned device login, 聪明脑袋模型 fallba…”。

原文小纸条

capabilities plus prepareRuntimeAuth and fetchUsageSnapshot because it needs provider-owned device login, model fallback behavior, Claude transcript quirks, a GitHub token -> Copilot token exchange, and a provider-owned usage endpoint.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“normalizeResolved聪明脑袋模型, refreshOAuth, and augment聪明脑袋模型Catalog plus prepareExtraParams, resolveUsageAuth, and fetchUsa…”。

原文小纸条

normalizeResolvedModel, refreshOAuth, and augmentModelCatalog plus prepareExtraParams, resolveUsageAuth, and fetchUsageSnapshot because it still runs on core OpenAI transports but owns its transport/base URL normalization, OAuth refresh fallback policy, default transport choice, synthetic Codex catalog rows, and ChatGPT usage endpoint integration.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“isModern聪明脑袋模型Ref because they own Gemini 3.1 forward-compat fallback and modern-聪明脑袋模型 matching; Gemini CLI OAuth also…”。

原文小纸条

isModernModelRef because they own Gemini 3.1 forward-compat fallback and modern-model matching; Gemini CLI OAuth also uses formatApiKey, resolveUsageAuth, and fetchUsageSnapshot for token formatting, token parsing, and quota endpoint wiring.

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“OpenAI transport but needs provider-owned thinking payload normalization.”。

isCacheTtlEligible because it needs provider-owned request headers, reasoning payload normalization, Gemini transcript hints, and Anthropic cache-TTL gating.

原文小纸条

isCacheTtlEligible, isBinaryThinking, isModernModelRef, resolveUsageAuth, and fetchUsageSnapshot because it owns GLM-5 fallback, tool_stream defaults, binary thinking UX, modern-model matching, and both usage auth + quota fetching.

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“huggingface, kimi-coding, 聪明脑袋模型studio, nvidia, qianfan, synthetic, together, venice, vercel-ai-门口的小门卫, and volcengine…”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“behavior is 新本领插件-owned even though inference still runs through the shared transports.”。

原文小纸条

behavior is plugin-owned even though inference still runs through the shared transports.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Built-in examples”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Anthropic uses resolveDynamic聪明脑袋模型, capa…、OpenAI uses resolveDynamic聪明脑袋模型, normali…、OpenRouter uses catalog plus resolveDynam…、GitHub Copilot uses catalog, auth, resolv…。

原文小纸条
  • Anthropic uses resolveDynamicModel, capabilities, buildAuthDoctorHint,
  • OpenAI uses resolveDynamicModel, normalizeResolvedModel, and
  • OpenRouter uses catalog plus resolveDynamicModel and
  • GitHub Copilot uses catalog, auth, resolveDynamicModel, and
  • OpenAI Codex uses catalog, resolveDynamicModel,
  • Google AI Studio and Gemini CLI OAuth use resolveDynamicModel and
  • Moonshot uses catalog plus wrapStreamFn because it still uses the shared
  • Kilocode uses catalog, capabilities, wrapStreamFn, and
  • Z.AI uses resolveDynamicModel, prepareExtraParams, wrapStreamFn,
  • Mistral, OpenCode Zen, and OpenCode Go use capabilities only to keep
  • Catalog-only bundled providers such as byteplus, cloudflare-ai-gateway,
  • Qwen portal uses catalog, auth, and refreshOAuth.
  • MiniMax and Xiaomi use catalog plus usage hooks because their /usage

第 26 站

Runtime helpers

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Runtime helpers”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“新本领插件s can access selected core helpers via 对话接口.runtime. For TTS:”。

原文小纸条

Plugins can access selected core helpers via api.runtime. For TTS:

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“const clip = await api.runtime.tts.textToSpeech({”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这里在给“text”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“Hello from OpenClaw”。
  • 这里在给“cfg”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“api.config”。
原文代码块
const clip = await api.runtime.tts.textToSpeech({
  text: "Hello from OpenClaw",
  cfg: api.config,
});

const result = await api.runtime.tts.textToSpeechTelephony({
  text: "Hello from OpenClaw",
  cfg: api.config,
});

const voices = await api.runtime.tts.listVoices({
  provider: "elevenlabs",
  cfg: api.config,
});
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Notes:”。

原文小纸条

Notes:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Runtime helpers”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：textToSpeech returns the normal core TTS…、Uses core 小纸条消息s.tts 设置说明书uration and pro…、Returns PCM audio buffer + sample rate. 新…、listVoices is optional per provider. Use…。

原文小纸条
  • textToSpeech returns the normal core TTS output payload for file/voice-note surfaces.
  • Uses core messages.tts configuration and provider selection.
  • Returns PCM audio buffer + sample rate. Plugins must resample/encode for providers.
  • listVoices is optional per provider. Use it for vendor-owned voice pickers or setup flows.
  • Voice listings can include richer metadata such as locale, gender, and personality tags for provider-aware pickers.
  • OpenAI and ElevenLabs support telephony today. Microsoft does not.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“新本领插件s can also register speech providers via 对话接口.registerSpeechProvider(...).”。

原文小纸条

Plugins can also register speech providers via api.registerSpeechProvider(...).

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“api.registerSpeechProvider({”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这里在给“id”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“acme-speech”。
  • 这里在给“label”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“Acme Speech”。
原文代码块
api.registerSpeechProvider({
  id: "acme-speech",
  label: "Acme Speech",
  isConfigured: ({ config }) => Boolean(config.messages?.tts),
  synthesize: async (req) => {
    return {
      audioBuffer: Buffer.from([]),
      outputFormat: "mp3",
      fileExtension: ".mp3",
      voiceCompatible: false,
    };
  },
});
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Notes:”。

Notes:

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“text, speech, image, and future media providers as OpenClaw adds those capability contracts.”。

原文小纸条

text, speech, image, and future media providers as OpenClaw adds those capability contracts.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Runtime helpers”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Keep TTS policy, fallback, and reply deli…、Use speech providers for vendor-owned syn…、Legacy Microsoft edge input is normalized…、The preferred ownership 聪明脑袋模型 is company…。

原文小纸条
  • Keep TTS policy, fallback, and reply delivery in core.
  • Use speech providers for vendor-owned synthesis behavior.
  • Legacy Microsoft edge input is normalized to the microsoft provider id.
  • The preferred ownership model is company-oriented: one vendor plugin can own
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“For image/audio/video understanding, 新本领插件s register one typed media-understanding provider instead of a generic key/va…”。

原文小纸条

For image/audio/video understanding, plugins register one typed media-understanding provider instead of a generic key/value bag:

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“api.registerMediaUnderstandingProvider({”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这里在给“id”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“google”。
  • 这里在给“capabilities”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“[image audio video]”。
原文代码块
api.registerMediaUnderstandingProvider({
  id: "google",
  capabilities: ["image", "audio", "video"],
  describeImage: async (req) => ({ text: "..." }),
  transcribeAudio: async (req) => ({ text: "..." }),
  describeVideo: async (req) => ({ text: "..." }),
});

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Runtime helpers”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Keep orchestration, fallback, 设置说明书, and…、Keep vendor behavior in the provider 新本领插…、Additive expansion should stay typed: new…、If OpenClaw adds a new capability such as…。

原文小纸条
  • Keep orchestration, fallback, config, and channel wiring in core.
  • Keep vendor behavior in the provider plugin.
  • Additive expansion should stay typed: new optional methods, new optional
  • If OpenClaw adds a new capability such as video generation later, define the
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“For media-understanding runtime helpers, 新本领插件s can call:”。

原文小纸条

For media-understanding runtime helpers, plugins can call:

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“const image = await api.runtime.mediaUnderstanding.describeImageFile({”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这里在给“filePath”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“/tmp/inbound-photo.jpg”。
  • 这里在给“cfg”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“api.config”。
原文代码块
const image = await api.runtime.mediaUnderstanding.describeImageFile({
  filePath: "/tmp/inbound-photo.jpg",
  cfg: api.config,
  agentDir: "/tmp/agent",
});

const video = await api.runtime.mediaUnderstanding.describeVideoFile({
  filePath: "/tmp/inbound-video.mp4",
  cfg: api.config,
});

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“For audio transcription, 新本领插件s can use either the media-understanding runtime or the older STT alias:”。

原文小纸条

For audio transcription, plugins can use either the media-understanding runtime or the older STT alias:

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“const { text } = await api.runtime.mediaUnderstanding.transcribeAudioFile({”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这里在给“filePath”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“/tmp/inbound-audio.ogg”。
  • 这里在给“cfg”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“api.config”。
原文代码块
const { text } = await api.runtime.mediaUnderstanding.transcribeAudioFile({
  filePath: "/tmp/inbound-audio.ogg",
  cfg: api.config,
  // Optional when MIME cannot be inferred reliably:
  mime: "audio/ogg",
});

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Runtime helpers”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：对话接口.runtime.mediaUnderstanding.* is the…、Uses core media-understanding audio 设置说明书…、Returns { text: undefined } when no trans…、对话接口.runtime.stt.transcribeAudioFile(...)…。

原文小纸条
  • api.runtime.mediaUnderstanding.* is the preferred shared surface for
  • Uses core media-understanding audio configuration (tools.media.audio) and provider fallback order.
  • Returns { text: undefined } when no transcription output is produced (for example skipped/unsupported input).
  • api.runtime.stt.transcribeAudioFile(...) remains as a compatibility alias.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“新本领插件s can also launch background sub机器人朋友 runs through 对话接口.runtime.sub机器人朋友:”。

原文小纸条

Plugins can also launch background subagent runs through api.runtime.subagent:

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“const result = await api.runtime.subagent.run({”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这里在给“sessionKey”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“agent:main:subagent:search-helper”。
  • 这里在给“message”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“Expand this query into focused follow-up searches.”。
原文代码块
const result = await api.runtime.subagent.run({
  sessionKey: "agent:main:subagent:search-helper",
  message: "Expand this query into focused follow-up searches.",
  provider: "openai",
  model: "gpt-4.1-mini",
  deliver: false,
});
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Notes:”。

原文小纸条

Notes:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Runtime helpers”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：provider and 聪明脑袋模型 are optional per-run…、OpenClaw only honors those override field…、For 新本领插件-owned fallback runs, operators…、Use 新本领插件s.entries.<id>.sub机器人朋友.allowed聪…。

原文小纸条
  • provider and model are optional per-run overrides, not persistent session changes.
  • OpenClaw only honors those override fields for trusted callers.
  • For plugin-owned fallback runs, operators must opt in with plugins.entries.<id>.subagent.allowModelOverride: true.
  • Use plugins.entries.<id>.subagent.allowedModels to restrict trusted plugins to specific canonical provider/model targets, or "*" to allow any target explicitly.
  • Untrusted plugin subagent runs still work, but override requests are rejected instead of silently falling back.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“For web search, 新本领插件s can consume the shared runtime helper instead of reaching into the 机器人朋友 工具小帮手 wiring:”。

原文小纸条

For web search, plugins can consume the shared runtime helper instead of reaching into the agent tool wiring:

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“const providers = api.runtime.webSearch.listProviders({”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这里在给“config”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“api.config”。
  • 大括号像把同一组设置抱在一起，说“这些是一家的”。
原文代码块
const providers = api.runtime.webSearch.listProviders({
  config: api.config,
});

const result = await api.runtime.webSearch.search({
  config: api.config,
  args: {
    query: "OpenClaw plugin runtime helpers",
    count: 5,
  },
});
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“新本领插件s can also register web-search providers via 对话接口.registerWebSearchProvider(...).”。

原文小纸条

Plugins can also register web-search providers via api.registerWebSearchProvider(...).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Notes:”。

原文小纸条

Notes:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Runtime helpers”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Keep provider selection, credential resol…、Use web-search providers for vendor-speci…、对话接口.runtime.webSearch.* is the preferred…。

原文小纸条
  • Keep provider selection, credential resolution, and shared request semantics in core.
  • Use web-search providers for vendor-specific search transports.
  • api.runtime.webSearch.* is the preferred shared surface for feature/channel plugins that need search behavior without depending on the agent tool wrapper.

第 27 站

门口的小门卫 HTTP routes

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“门口的小门卫 HTTP routes”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“新本领插件s can expose HTTP endpoints with 对话接口.registerHttpRoute(...).”。

原文小纸条

Plugins can expose HTTP endpoints with api.registerHttpRoute(...).

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“api.registerHttpRoute({”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这里在给“path”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“/acme/webhook”。
  • 这里在给“auth”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“plugin”。
原文代码块
api.registerHttpRoute({
  path: "/acme/webhook",
  auth: "plugin",
  match: "exact",
  handler: async (_req, res) => {
    res.statusCode = 200;
    res.end("ok");
    return true;
  },
});
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Route fields:”。

原文小纸条

Route fields:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Gateway HTTP routes”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：path: route path under the 门口的小门卫 HTTP 大房…、auth: required. Use "门口的小门卫" to require n…、match: optional. "exact" (default) or "pr…、replaceExisting: optional. Allows the sam…。

原文小纸条
  • path: route path under the gateway HTTP server.
  • auth: required. Use "gateway" to require normal gateway auth, or "plugin" for plugin-managed auth/webhook verification.
  • match: optional. "exact" (default) or "prefix".
  • replaceExisting: optional. Allows the same plugin to replace its own existing route registration.
  • handler: return true when the route handled the request.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Notes:”。

原文小纸条

Notes:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Gateway HTTP routes”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：对话接口.registerHttpHandler(...) is obsolete…、新本领插件 routes must declare auth explicitly.、Exact path + match conflicts are rejected…、Overlapping routes with different auth le…。

原文小纸条
  • api.registerHttpHandler(...) is obsolete. Use api.registerHttpRoute(...).
  • Plugin routes must declare auth explicitly.
  • Exact path + match conflicts are rejected unless replaceExisting: true, and one plugin cannot replace another plugin's route.
  • Overlapping routes with different auth levels are rejected. Keep exact/prefix fallthrough chains on the same auth level only.

新本领插件 SDK import paths

原文小纸条

Use SDK subpaths instead of the monolithic openclaw/plugin-sdk import when authoring plugins:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“openclaw/新本领插件-sdk/消息通道-pairing, openclaw/新本领插件-sdk/消息通道-contract, openclaw/新本领插件-sdk/消息通道-feedback, openclaw/新本领插件-sdk…”。

原文小纸条

openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-pairing, openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-contract, openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-feedback, openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-inbound, openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-lifecycle, openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-reply-pipeline, openclaw/plugin-sdk/command-auth, openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input, and openclaw/plugin-sdk/webhook-ingress for shared setup/auth/reply/webhook wiring. channel-inbound is the shared home for debounce, mention matching, envelope formatting, and inbound envelope context helpers.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“openclaw/新本领插件-sdk/allow-from, openclaw/新本领插件-sdk/消息通道-设置说明书-schema, openclaw/新本领插件-sdk/消息通道-policy, openclaw/新本领插件-sdk…”。

原文小纸条

openclaw/plugin-sdk/allow-from, openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema, openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-policy, openclaw/plugin-sdk/config-runtime, openclaw/plugin-sdk/infra-runtime, openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-runtime, openclaw/plugin-sdk/lazy-runtime, openclaw/plugin-sdk/reply-history, openclaw/plugin-sdk/routing, openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers, openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store, and openclaw/plugin-sdk/directory-runtime for shared runtime/config helpers.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“New code should import the narrower primitives instead.”。

原文小纸条

New code should import the narrower primitives instead.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“openclaw/新本领插件-sdk/* subpaths. OpenClaw core/test code may use the repo public entry points under extensions/<id>/index…”。

原文小纸条

openclaw/plugin-sdk/* subpaths. OpenClaw core/test code may use the repo public entry points under extensions/<id>/index.js, api.js, runtime-api.js, setup-entry.js, and narrowly scoped files such as login-qr-api.js. Never import extensions/<id>/src/* from core or from another extension.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“extensions/<id>/对话接口.js is the helper/types barrel, extensions/<id>/runtime-对话接口.js is the runtime-only barrel, extensi…”。

原文小纸条

extensions/<id>/api.js is the helper/types barrel, extensions/<id>/runtime-api.js is the runtime-only barrel, extensions/<id>/index.js is the bundled plugin entry, and extensions/<id>/setup-entry.js is the setup plugin entry.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“runtime seams live under extensions/<id>/对话接口.js and extensions/<id>/runtime-对话接口.js; the public SDK contract is the ge…”。

原文小纸条

runtime seams live under extensions/<id>/api.js and extensions/<id>/runtime-api.js; the public SDK contract is the generic shared primitives instead.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Plugin SDK import paths”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：openclaw/新本领插件-sdk/新本领插件-entry for 新本领插件…、openclaw/新本领插件-sdk/core for the generic s…、Stable 消息通道 primitives such as openclaw/新…、Domain subpaths such as openclaw/新本领插件-sd…。

原文小纸条
  • openclaw/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry for plugin registration primitives.
  • openclaw/plugin-sdk/core for the generic shared plugin-facing contract.
  • Stable channel primitives such as openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-setup,
  • Domain subpaths such as openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers,
  • openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-runtime remains only as a compatibility shim.
  • Bundled extension internals remain private. External plugins should use only
  • Repo entry point split:
  • No bundled channel-branded public subpaths remain. Channel-specific helper and

feedback/contract/inbound/threading/command/secret-input/webhook/infra/ allowlist/status/message-tool subpaths are the intended contract for new bundled and external plugin work. Target parsing/matching belongs on openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-targets. Message action gates and reaction message-id helpers belong on openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-actions.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Plugin SDK import paths”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Avoid the root openclaw/新本领插件-sdk barrel…、Prefer the narrow stable primitives first…、Bundled extension-specific helper barrels…、消息通道-branded bundled bars stay private un…。

原文小纸条
  • Avoid the root openclaw/plugin-sdk barrel for new code.
  • Prefer the narrow stable primitives first. The newer setup/pairing/reply/
  • Bundled extension-specific helper barrels are not stable by default. If a
  • Channel-branded bundled bars stay private unless they are explicitly added

  • messaging.targetResolver.resolveTarget(...) is the plugin fallback when
  • messaging.resolveOutboundSessionRoute(...) owns provider-specific session

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Channel target resolution”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Use inferTargetChatType for category deci…、Use looksLikeId for "treat this as an exp…、Use resolveTarget for provider-specific n…、Keep provider-native ids like chat ids, t…。

原文小纸条
  • Use inferTargetChatType for category decisions that should happen before
  • Use looksLikeId for "treat this as an explicit/native target id" checks.
  • Use resolveTarget for provider-specific normalization fallback, not for
  • Keep provider-native ids like chat ids, thread ids, JIDs, handles, and room

catalog.order controls when a plugin's catalog merges relative to OpenClaw's built-in implicit providers:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Provider catalogs”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：simple: plain 对话接口-key or env-driven prov…、profile: providers that appear when auth…、paired: providers that synthesize multipl…、late: last pass, after other implicit pro…。

原文小纸条
  • simple: plain API-key or env-driven providers
  • profile: providers that appear when auth profiles exist
  • paired: providers that synthesize multiple related provider entries
  • late: last pass, after other implicit providers
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Later providers win on key collision, so 新本领插件s can intentionally override a built-in provider entry with the same prov…”。

原文小纸条

Later providers win on key collision, so plugins can intentionally override a built-in provider entry with the same provider id.

像讲绘本

Compatibility:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Provider catalogs”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：discovery still works as a legacy alias、if both catalog and discovery are registe…。

原文小纸条
  • discovery still works as a legacy alias
  • if both catalog and discovery are registered, OpenClaw uses catalog

are fully materialized and can fail fast when required secrets are missing.

openclaw channels status, openclaw channels resolve, and doctor/config repair flows should not need to materialize runtime credentials just to describe configuration.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Read-only channel inspection”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：resolveAccount(...) is the runtime path.…、Read-only 魔法口令 paths such as openclaw sta…。

  • resolveAccount(...) is the runtime path. It is allowed to assume credentials
  • Read-only command paths such as openclaw status, openclaw status --all,

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“availability. Returning tokenStatus: "available" (and the matching source field) is enough for status-style 魔法口令s.”。

原文小纸条

availability. Returning tokenStatus: "available" (and the matching source field) is enough for status-style commands.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“unavailable in the current 魔法口令 path.”。

unavailable in the current command path.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Read-only channel inspection”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Return descriptive account state only.、Preserve enabled and 设置说明书ured.、Include credential source/status fields w…、tokenSource, tokenStatus。

原文小纸条
  • Return descriptive account state only.
  • Preserve enabled and configured.
  • Include credential source/status fields when relevant, such as:
  • tokenSource, tokenStatus
  • botTokenSource, botTokenStatus
  • appTokenSource, appTokenStatus
  • signingSecretSource, signingSecretStatus
  • You do not need to return raw token values just to report read-only
  • Use configured_unavailable when a credential is configured via SecretRef but
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“This lets read-only 魔法口令s report "设置说明书ured but unavailable in this 魔法口令 path" instead of crashing or misreporting the…”。

原文小纸条

This lets read-only commands report "configured but unavailable in this command path" instead of crashing or misreporting the account as not configured.

第 34 站

Package packs

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Package packs”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“A 新本领插件 directory may include a package.json with openclaw.extensions:”。

原文小纸条

A plugin directory may include a package.json with openclaw.extensions:

像魔法口令拆解

这段不是故事对白，而是设置卡片，像在给机器人贴名字、地址和规则标签。

  • 大括号像把同一组设置抱在一起，说“这些是一家的”。
  • 这里在给“name”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“my-pack”。
  • 这里在给“openclaw”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“{”。
原文代码块
{
  "name": "my-pack",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./src/safety.ts", "./src/tools.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./src/setup-entry.ts"
  }
}
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Each entry becomes a 新本领插件. If the pack lists multiple extensions, the 新本领插件 id becomes name/<fileBase>.”。

原文小纸条

Each entry becomes a plugin. If the pack lists multiple extensions, the plugin id becomes name/<fileBase>.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If your 新本领插件 imports npm deps, install them in that directory so node_modules is available (npm install / pnpm install…”。

原文小纸条

If your plugin imports npm deps, install them in that directory so node_modules is available (npm install / pnpm install).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“安全守门员 guardrail: every openclaw.extensions entry must stay inside the 新本领插件 directory after symlink resolution. Entries…”。

原文小纸条

Security guardrail: every openclaw.extensions entry must stay inside the plugin directory after symlink resolution. Entries that escape the package directory are rejected.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“安全守门员 note: openclaw 新本领插件s install installs 新本领插件 dependencies with npm install --ignore-scripts (no lifecycle scripts…”。

原文小纸条

Security note: openclaw plugins install installs plugin dependencies with npm install --ignore-scripts (no lifecycle scripts). Keep plugin dependency trees "pure JS/TS" and avoid packages that require postinstall builds.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Optional: openclaw.setupEntry can point at a lightweight setup-only module. When OpenClaw needs setup surfaces for a di…”。

原文小纸条

Optional: openclaw.setupEntry can point at a lightweight setup-only module. When OpenClaw needs setup surfaces for a disabled channel plugin, or when a channel plugin is enabled but still unconfigured, it loads setupEntry instead of the full plugin entry. This keeps startup and setup lighter when your main plugin entry also wires tools, hooks, or other runtime-only code.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Optional: openclaw.startup.defer设置说明书ured消息通道FullLoadUntilAfterListen can opt a 消息通道 新本领插件 into the same setupEntry pat…”。

原文小纸条

Optional: openclaw.startup.deferConfiguredChannelFullLoadUntilAfterListen can opt a channel plugin into the same setupEntry path during the gateway's pre-listen startup phase, even when the channel is already configured.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Use this only when setupEntry fully covers the startup surface that must exist before the 门口的小门卫 starts listening. In p…”。

原文小纸条

Use this only when setupEntry fully covers the startup surface that must exist before the gateway starts listening. In practice, that means the setup entry must register every channel-owned capability that startup depends on, such as:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Package packs”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：消息通道 registration itself、any HTTP routes that must be available be…、any 门口的小门卫 methods, 工具小帮手s, or services t…。

原文小纸条
  • channel registration itself
  • any HTTP routes that must be available before the gateway starts listening
  • any gateway methods, tools, or services that must exist during that same window
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If your full entry still owns any required startup capability, do not enable this flag. Keep the 新本领插件 on the default b…”。

原文小纸条

If your full entry still owns any required startup capability, do not enable this flag. Keep the plugin on the default behavior and let OpenClaw load the full entry during startup.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Example:”。

原文小纸条

Example:

像魔法口令拆解

这段不是故事对白，而是设置卡片，像在给机器人贴名字、地址和规则标签。

  • 大括号像把同一组设置抱在一起，说“这些是一家的”。
  • 这里在给“name”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“@scope/my-channel”。
  • 这里在给“openclaw”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“{”。
原文代码块
{
  "name": "@scope/my-channel",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "startup": {
      "deferConfiguredChannelFullLoadUntilAfterListen": true
    }
  }
}

消息通道 catalog metadata

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“消息通道 catalog metadata”。

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“消息通道 新本领插件s can advertise setup/discovery metadata via openclaw.消息通道 and install hints via openclaw.install. This keeps…”。

原文小纸条

Channel plugins can advertise setup/discovery metadata via openclaw.channel and install hints via openclaw.install. This keeps the core catalog data-free.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Example:”。

原文小纸条

Example:

像魔法口令拆解

这段不是故事对白，而是设置卡片，像在给机器人贴名字、地址和规则标签。

  • 大括号像把同一组设置抱在一起，说“这些是一家的”。
  • 这里在给“name”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“@openclaw/nextcloud-talk”。
  • 这里在给“openclaw”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“{”。
原文代码块
{
  "name": "@openclaw/nextcloud-talk",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "channel": {
      "id": "nextcloud-talk",
      "label": "Nextcloud Talk",
      "selectionLabel": "Nextcloud Talk (self-hosted)",
      "docsPath": "/channels/nextcloud-talk",
      "docsLabel": "nextcloud-talk",
      "blurb": "Self-hosted chat via Nextcloud Talk webhook bots.",
      "order": 65,
      "aliases": ["nc-talk", "nc"]
    },
    "install": {
      "npmSpec": "@openclaw/nextcloud-talk",
      "localPath": "extensions/nextcloud-talk",
      "defaultChoice": "npm"
    }
  }
}
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“OpenClaw can also merge **external 消息通道 catalogs** (for example, an MPM registry export). Drop a JSON file at one of:”。

原文小纸条

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Channel catalog metadata”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：~/.openclaw/mpm/新本领插件s.json、~/.openclaw/mpm/catalog.json、~/.openclaw/新本领插件s/catalog.json。

原文小纸条
  • ~/.openclaw/mpm/plugins.json
  • ~/.openclaw/mpm/catalog.json

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Or point OPENCLAW_新本领插件_CATALOG_PATHS (or OPENCLAW_MPM_CATALOG_PATHS) at one or more JSON files (comma/semicolon/PATH-d…”。

原文小纸条

Or point OPENCLAW_PLUGIN_CATALOG_PATHS (or OPENCLAW_MPM_CATALOG_PATHS) at one or more JSON files (comma/semicolon/PATH-delimited). Each file should contain { "entries": [ { "name": "@scope/pkg", "openclaw": { "channel": {...}, "install": {...} } } ] }.

第 36 站

Context engine 新本领插件s

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Context engine 新本领插件s”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Context engine 新本领插件s own session context orchestration for ingest, assembly, and compaction. Register them from your 新…”。

原文小纸条

Context engine plugins own session context orchestration for ingest, assembly, and compaction. Register them from your plugin with api.registerContextEngine(id, factory), then select the active engine with plugins.slots.contextEngine.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Use this when your 新本领插件 needs to replace or extend the default context pipeline rather than just add memory search or…”。

原文小纸条

Use this when your plugin needs to replace or extend the default context pipeline rather than just add memory search or hooks.

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“export default function (api) {”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这一行“api.registerContextEngine("lossless-claw", () => ({”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这里在给“info”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“{ id: lossless-claw name: Lossless Claw ownsCompaction: true }”。
原文代码块
export default function (api) {
  api.registerContextEngine("lossless-claw", () => ({
    info: { id: "lossless-claw", name: "Lossless Claw", ownsCompaction: true },
    async ingest() {
      return { ingested: true };
    },
    async assemble({ messages }) {
      return { messages, estimatedTokens: 0 };
    },
    async compact() {
      return { ok: true, compacted: false };
    },
  }));
}
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If your engine does **not** own the compaction algorithm, keep compact() implemented and delegate it explicitly:”。

原文小纸条

If your engine does **not** own the compaction algorithm, keep compact() implemented and delegate it explicitly:

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“import { delegateCompactionToRuntime } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这一行“export default function (api) {”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这一行“api.registerContextEngine("my-memory-engine", () => ({”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
原文代码块
import { delegateCompactionToRuntime } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";

export default function (api) {
  api.registerContextEngine("my-memory-engine", () => ({
    info: {
      id: "my-memory-engine",
      name: "My Memory Engine",
      ownsCompaction: false,
    },
    async ingest() {
      return { ingested: true };
    },
    async assemble({ messages }) {
      return { messages, estimatedTokens: 0 };
    },
    async compact(params) {
      return await delegateCompactionToRuntime(params);
    },
  }));
}

第 37 站

Adding a new capability

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Adding a new capability”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“When a 新本领插件 needs behavior that does not fit the current 对话接口, do not bypass the 新本领插件 system with a private reach-in.…”。

原文小纸条

When a plugin needs behavior that does not fit the current API, do not bypass the plugin system with a private reach-in. Add the missing capability.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Recommended sequence:”。

原文小纸条

Recommended sequence:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. define the core contract Decide what shared behavior core should own: policy, fallback, 设置说明书 merge, lifecycle, 消息通道…”。

原文小纸条

1. define the core contract Decide what shared behavior core should own: policy, fallback, config merge, lifecycle, channel-facing semantics, and runtime helper shape. 2. add typed plugin registration/runtime surfaces Extend OpenClawPluginApi and/or api.runtime with the smallest useful typed capability surface. 3. wire core + channel/feature consumers Channels and feature plugins should consume the new capability through core, not by importing a vendor implementation directly. 4. register vendor implementations Vendor plugins then register their backends against the capability. 5. add contract coverage Add tests so ownership and registration shape stay explicit over time.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“This is how OpenClaw stays opinionated without becoming hardcoded to one provider's worldview. See the Capability Cookb…”。

原文小纸条

This is how OpenClaw stays opinionated without becoming hardcoded to one provider's worldview. See the Capability Cookbook for a concrete file checklist and worked example.

第 38 站

Capability checklist

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Capability checklist”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“When you add a new capability, the implementation should usually touch these surfaces together:”。

原文小纸条

When you add a new capability, the implementation should usually touch these surfaces together:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“新本领插件s need to consume it”。

原文小纸条

plugins need to consume it

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Capability checklist”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：core contract types in src/<capability>/t…、core runner/runtime helper in src/<capabi…、新本领插件 对话接口 registration surface in src/新本…、新本领插件 registry wiring in src/新本领插件s/regis…。

原文小纸条
  • core contract types in src/<capability>/types.ts
  • core runner/runtime helper in src/<capability>/runtime.ts
  • plugin API registration surface in src/plugins/types.ts
  • plugin registry wiring in src/plugins/registry.ts
  • plugin runtime exposure in src/plugins/runtime/* when feature/channel
  • capture/test helpers in src/test-utils/plugin-registration.ts
  • ownership/contract assertions in src/plugins/contracts/registry.ts
  • operator/plugin docs in docs/
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If one of those surfaces is missing, that is usually a sign the capability is not fully integrated yet.”。

原文小纸条

If one of those surfaces is missing, that is usually a sign the capability is not fully integrated yet.

第 39 站

Capability template

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Capability template”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Minimal pattern:”。

原文小纸条

Minimal pattern:

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“// core contract”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这一行“export type VideoGenerationProviderPlugin = {”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
  • 这里在给“id”贴标签，意思是把它设置成“string;”。
原文代码块
// core contract
export type VideoGenerationProviderPlugin = {
  id: string;
  label: string;
  generateVideo: (req: VideoGenerationRequest) => Promise<VideoGenerationResult>;
};

// plugin API
api.registerVideoGenerationProvider({
  id: "openai",
  label: "OpenAI",
  async generateVideo(req) {
    return await generateOpenAiVideo(req);
  },
});

// shared runtime helper for feature/channel plugins
const clip = await api.runtime.videoGeneration.generateFile({
  prompt: "Show the robot walking through the lab.",
  cfg,
});
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Contract test pattern:”。

原文小纸条

Contract test pattern:

像魔法口令拆解

这段像给机器人看的说明书，告诉它要按什么样子准备东西。

  • 这一行“expect(findVideoGenerationProviderIdsForPlugin("openai")).toEqual(["openai"]);”是当前步骤要交给电脑的一小块提示。
原文代码块
expect(findVideoGenerationProviderIdsForPlugin("openai")).toEqual(["openai"]);
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“That keeps the rule simple:”。

原文小纸条

That keeps the rule simple:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Capability template”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：core owns the capability contract + orche…、vendor 新本领插件s own vendor implementations、feature/消息通道 新本领插件s consume runtime helpe…、contract tests keep ownership explicit。

原文小纸条
  • core owns the capability contract + orchestration
  • vendor plugins own vendor implementations
  • feature/channel plugins consume runtime helpers
  • contract tests keep ownership explicit

这里预留给未来的 Google AdSense 模块。
